The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification for pluck_spec. It checks all three conditions: (1) empty array returns empty result, (2) non-empty array with all odd elements returns empty result, (3) non-empty array with even elements returns [smallest_even, first_index_of_smallest_even]. The helper functions is_even, is_odd, all_odd, has_even, is_smallest_even, and is_first_index_of all correctly mirror their Coq counterparts for concrete inputs. The Python spec function returns True exactly when the result satisfies the Coq specification.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification for pluck_spec. It checks all three conditions: (1) empty array implies empty result, (2) non-empty array with all odd elements implies empty result, (3) non-empty array with even elements requires result to be [min_even, idx] where min_even is the smallest even element and idx is its first occurrence index. The helper functions is_even, is_odd, all_odd, has_even, is_smallest_even, and is_first_index_of all correctly implement their Coq counterparts for concrete inputs.
